findings.evidence: roadmap JacobianChallenge/README.md Layer A list (lines 92-100) contains no 'linear system'; grep for 'linear system'/'L(D)' over the roadmap returns no hit; Riemann–Roch appears only at Layer B (lines 102,106) as χ(L)=deg L+1−g; Principal.lean already provides divisorClass/weightedDegreeClass/picZero without |D|.
findings.fix: Drop from this PR; have a human add complete linear systems to JacobianChallenge/README.md (with a named consuming target) before building it.
findings.issue: |D| is a terminal leaf of the divisor theory with no proximate roadmap consumer: it is downstream of principal divisors/classes and is not a prerequisite for any Layer A target (Cl(X)≅Pic X, degree, Pic⁰=ker deg, all already built in Principal.lean). Its genuine consumer, the L(D)/ℙ(L(D)) Riemann–Roch theory, is Layer B (explicitly deferred), is stated cohomologically there, and cannot be expressed in the abstract-group OrderSystem model (no field, no functions).

summary: The complete linear system |D| is correct divisor theory but is not a named roadmap target and not a proximate prerequisite for any: no Layer A target (Cl≅Pic, degree, Pic⁰) consumes it, and its real consumer L(D)/Riemann–Roch is Layer B, deferred and inexpressible in this abstract-group OrderSystem model. A human must add complete linear systems to the roadmap before it is built here.
